title: I got tired of not knowing what my AI agents were doing, so I built a tiny observability tool
excerpt: I build small LLM agents. Not the impressive kind you see in demos, just practical little things that...
content_html:
I build small LLM agents. Not the impressive kind you see in demos, just
practical little things that answer support questions or dig through some
docs and come back with an answer. And for months the most annoying part of
that wasn't the model or the prompt. It was that I had no idea what the thing
was actually doing.

You know the feeling. A run takes nine seconds and you don't know why. It
called a tool, then called it again, then a third time, and somewhere in
there it spent forty cents. The logs are a wall of JSON. You tweak a prompt,
ship it, and you genuinely can't tell if you made it better or worse until a
user complains. I'd been squinting at print() output like it was 2015.

So I went looking for something to just show me my agent runs. And the tools
exist — they're fine, some are really good — but they all wanted something I
didn't want to give.

The two things that kept bugging me

The hosted ones want your traces on their servers. Which means your prompts,
which for me means actual customer messages, sitting in someone else's
database so I can look at a dashboard. No thanks.

The self-hostable ones are built for companies, and it shows. The one
everybody recommends needs Postgres and ClickHouse and Redis and an S3
bucket to run. Four stateful services. I'm logging maybe a few thousand model
calls a day on a side project. Standing up a ClickHouse cluster to look at
that is like renting a forklift to carry a bag of groceries.

I sat with that for a bit and realized the mismatch was the whole problem.
This isn't company-scale data. It's my-laptop-scale data. And the moment you
accept that, the design gets really boring in a good way: it's just a web app
with a SQLite file. That's it. One process.

So I built that. It's called Otterscope.

What it actually is

It's one Go binary. You run it, it opens a SQLite file, and it listens for
OpenTelemetry traces on the standard port. You point your agent at it with
one environment variable — the same one every OpenTelemetry setup already
uses — and your runs start showing up. No account, no config file, no other
services to babysit.

The thing I cared most about getting right was the shape of the data.
Everyone else shows you spans, which is the raw OpenTelemetry unit, and it
reads like a stack trace. I wanted runs. One agent execution is one run, and
inside it you see the steps: this LLM call, that tool call, the loop where it
called the same tool three times. Click into a call and you read the real
messages that went in and came back out, with the token counts and the cost
sitting right there. When something goes sideways, you can actually see where.

There's a cost table that knows the current prices for the major providers,
so a run tells you what it cost instead of just how many tokens it burned. If
it hits a model I don't have a price for, it shows you the tokens and stays
honest about not knowing the dollar amount, which matters more than it
sounds — I'd rather see a blank than a made-up number.

And then there's the part I'm most attached to: the evals live in the same
place as the traces. You write a check ("the answer should never contain 'I
can't help with that'") or point an LLM at the run as a judge, and the result
gets stamped onto the actual production run. Most tools make evals a separate
product you run against a separate dataset. Here it's just a column on the
runs you already have. There's a compare view too, so "did my prompt change
make last week worse than the week before" is a question you can actually
answer by looking, instead of by vibes.

The genuinely annoying part

If you build one of these, here's the thing nobody warns you about: there is
no single format for AI traces. There are at least three, all live in the
wild right now, and they disagree.

OpenTelemetry has a "GenAI" convention, except it's mid-migration, so there's
an old attribute layout and a new one and both are being emitted by real
tools today. Then Arize's OpenInference is its own thing, and it's what the
OpenAI Agents SDK and a bunch of the LangChain world speak. Then the Vercel
AI SDK does its own ai.* attributes, and — this is the fun one — its spans
mix the old and new OpenTelemetry styles together in the same span, so if you
guess based on one attribute you get it wrong.

So a big chunk of Otterscope is just a translation layer that eats all of
these and turns them into one clean model. It's the least glamorous code in
the project and probably the most valuable, because it means you don't have
to care which dialect your framework speaks. And because I keep the raw
payload of everything that comes in, when I improve that translation later,
you can replay your old data through the new version and it just gets better
retroactively. That felt worth doing.

What it's not

It's single-user right now. There's no login, no teams, no roles. That's on
purpose — I built the thing I needed, and I'm one person. It binds to
localhost by default so you're not accidentally exposing it, and if you want
a team version with real auth, that's a "when someone actually asks" feature,
not a "build it on spec because a spreadsheet said B2B" feature.

It's also young. It'll have rough edges. But it does the thing I wanted, which
was to stop flying blind.

Trying it

The fastest way is Docker:

docker run -p 8317:8317 -p 4318:4318 -v otterscope:/data ghcr.io/otterscope/otterscope

Then point any OpenTelemetry-instrumented agent at http://localhost:4318
and open http://localhost:8317. There's a sample command that fills it
with fake runs if you just want to click around first. The repo has setup
snippets for Pydantic AI, the OpenAI Agents SDK, LangGraph, and the Vercel AI
SDK.

Repo's here: https://github.com/otterscope

If you'd rather run it on a server than on your laptop, I wrote up the whole
thing on a small VPS separately — creating the box, Docker vs a systemd
service, and how to keep it private with an SSH tunnel or lock it down with a
firewall: Self-hosting observability for your AI agents on a $6
Droplet.

Why DigitalOcean, since a few people asked

Because the tool's whole personality is "small and predictable," and that's
what I want from the box it runs on too.

A $6 Droplet runs this comfortably. There's no managed database bill on top,
because the database is a file — I back it up by copying the file or
snapshotting the Droplet, and I'm done. The pricing is flat and I can do the
math in my head, which matters when the whole point of the project is not
having a surprise infrastructure bill for a side project. I don't want to
find out at the end of the month that some egress line item ate the savings.

I've also just had a good time on it. The docs are readable, the Droplet
comes up in under a minute, and the Marketplace Docker image means "spin up a
box that can run a container" is a checkbox rather than an afternoon. For
something like this — one binary, one file, one small server sitting next to
your agents — it fits the shape of the thing. That's really the whole reason.

Anyway. If you've been squinting at agent logs, go self-host something and
stop. It doesn't have to be mine, but it turns out it really doesn't need to
be complicated.

Everyone else shows you spans, which is the raw OpenTelemetry unit, and it reads like a stack trace. I wanted runs. Click into a call and you read the real messages that went in and came back out, with the token counts and the cost sitting right there. When something goes sideways, you can actually see where. There's a cost table that knows the current prices for the major providers, so a run tells you what it cost instead of just how many tokens it burned. And then there's the part I'm most attached to: the evals live in the same place as the traces. There's a compare view too, so "did my prompt change make last week worse than the week before" is a question you can actually answer by looking, instead of by vibes. Then Arize's OpenInference is its own thing, and it's what the OpenAI Agents SDK and a bunch of the LangChain world speak. Then the Vercel AI SDK does its own ai.* attributes, and — this is the fun one — its spans mix the old and new OpenTelemetry styles together in the same span, so if you guess based on one attribute you get it wrong. So a big chunk of Otterscope is just a translation layer that eats all of these and turns them into one clean model. It's the least glamorous code in the project and probably the most valuable, because it means you don't have to care which dialect your framework speaks. And because I keep the raw payload of everything that comes in, when I improve that translation later, you can replay your old data through the new version and it just gets better retroactively. That felt worth doing. What it's not It's single-user right now. There's no login, no teams, no roles. That's on purpose — I built the thing I needed, and I'm one person. It binds to localhost by default so you're not accidentally exposing it, and if you want a team version with real auth, that's a "when someone actually asks" feature, not a "build it on spec because a spreadsheet said B2B" feature. guess based on one attribute you get it wrong.

sentences.en: OpenTelemetry has a "GenAI" convention, except it's mid-migration, so there's an old attribute layout and a new one and both are being emitted by real tools today.
sentences.zh: OpenTelemetry 有一个“GenAI”约定，只是它处于迁移中期，因此有一个旧的属性布局和一个新的属性布局，并且两者都由当今的真实工具发出。

sentences.en: Then Arize's OpenInference is its own thing, and it's what the OpenAI Agents SDK and a bunch of the LangChain world speak.
sentences.zh: 然后Arize的OpenInference是它自己的东西，它是OpenAI Agents SDK和一堆LangChain世界所说的东西。

sentences.en: Then the Vercel AI SDK does its own ai.* attributes, and — this is the fun one — its spans mix the old and new OpenTelemetry styles together in the same span, so if you guess based on one attribute you get it wrong.
sentences.zh: 然后，Vercel AI SDK 会执行自己的 ai.* 属性，并且（这是有趣的）它的跨度将新旧 OpenTelemetry 样式混合在同一个跨度中，因此，如果您根据一个属性进行猜测，您就会出错。

blocks.html_en:
So a big chunk of Otterscope is just a translation layer that eats all of
these and turns them into one clean model. It's the least glamorous code in
the project and probably the most valuable, because it means you don't have
to care which dialect your framework speaks. And because I keep the raw
payload of everything that comes in, when I improve that translation later,
you can replay your old data through the new version and it just gets better
retroactively. That felt worth doing.

sentences.en: So a big chunk of Otterscope is just a translation layer that eats all of these and turns them into one clean model.
sentences.zh: 因此，Otterscope 的很大一部分只是一个翻译层，它吞噬所有这些并将它们转变为一个干净的模型。

sentences.en: It's the least glamorous code in the project and probably the most valuable, because it means you don't have to care which dialect your framework speaks.
sentences.zh: 它是项目中最不引人注目的代码，但可能也是最有价值的，因为这意味着您不必关心您的框架使用哪种方言。

sentences.en: And because I keep the raw payload of everything that comes in, when I improve that translation later, you can replay your old data through the new version and it just gets better retroactively.
sentences.zh: 而且因为我保留了所有进来的原始有效负载，当我稍后改进翻译时，您可以通过新版本重播旧数据，并且回溯性会变得更好。

sentences.en: That felt worth doing.
sentences.zh: 那感觉值得做。

blocks.html_en: What it's not

sentences.en: What it's not
sentences.zh: 它不是什么

blocks.html_en:
It's single-user right now. There's no login, no teams, no roles. That's on
purpose — I built the thing I needed, and I'm one person. It binds to
localhost by default so you're not accidentally exposing it, and if you want
a team version with real auth, that's a "when someone actually asks" feature,
not a "build it on spec because a spreadsheet said B2B" feature.

sentences.en: It's single-user right now.
sentences.zh: 现在是单用户。

sentences.en: There's no login, no teams, no roles.
sentences.zh: 没有登录、没有团队、没有角色。

sentences.en: That's on purpose — I built the thing I needed, and I'm one person.
sentences.zh: 这是故意的——我建造了我需要的东西，而且我是一个人。

sentences.en: It binds to localhost by default so you're not accidentally exposing it, and if you want a team version with real auth, that's a "when someone actually asks" feature, not a "build it on spec because a spreadsheet said B2B" feature.
sentences.zh: 它默认绑定到本地主机，这样你就不会意外暴露它，如果你想要一个具有真正身份验证的团队版本，这是一个“当有人真正询问时”功能，而不是“根据规范构建它，因为电子表格说 B2B”功能。

blocks.html_en:
It's also young. It'll have rough edges. But it does the thing I wanted, which
was to stop flying blind.

sentences.en: It's also young.
sentences.zh: 也很年轻。

sentences.en: It'll have rough edges.
sentences.zh: 它会有粗糙的边缘。

sentences.en: But it does the thing I wanted, which was to stop flying blind.
sentences.zh: 但它做到了我想要的事情，那就是停止盲目飞行。

blocks.html_en: Trying it

sentences.en: Trying it
sentences.zh: 尝试一下

blocks.html_en: The fastest way is Docker:

sentences.en: The fastest way is Docker:
sentences.zh: 最快的方法是 Docker：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en:
Then point any OpenTelemetry-instrumented agent at http://localhost:4318
and open http://localhost:8317. There's a sample command that fills it
with fake runs if you just want to click around first. The repo has setup
snippets for Pydantic AI, the OpenAI Agents SDK, LangGraph, and the Vercel AI
SDK.

sentences.en: Then point any OpenTelemetry-instrumented agent at http://localhost:4318 and open http://localhost:8317. There's a sample command that fills it with fake runs if you just want to click around first.
sentences.zh: 然后将任何 OpenTelemetry 检测的代理指向 http://localhost:4318 并打开 http://localhost:8317。如果您只想先单击一下，有一个示例命令可以用假运行填充它。

sentences.en: The repo has setup snippets for Pydantic AI, the OpenAI Agents SDK, LangGraph, and the Vercel AI SDK.
sentences.zh: 该存储库包含 Pydantic AI、OpenAI Agents SDK、LangGraph 和 Vercel AI SDK 的设置片段。

blocks.html_en: Repo's here: https://github.com/otterscope

sentences.en: Repo's here: https://github.com/otterscope
sentences.zh: 回购协议在这里：https://github.com/otterscope

blocks.html_en:
If you'd rather run it on a server than on your laptop, I wrote up the whole
thing on a small VPS separately — creating the box, Docker vs a systemd
service, and how to keep it private with an SSH tunnel or lock it down with a
firewall: Self-hosting observability for your AI agents on a $6
Droplet.

sentences.en: If you'd rather run it on a server than on your laptop, I wrote up the whole thing on a small VPS separately — creating the box, Docker vs a systemd service, and how to keep it private with an SSH tunnel or lock it down with a firewall: Self-hosting observability for your AI agents on a $6 Droplet.
sentences.zh: 如果你更愿意在服务器上运行它而不是在笔记本电脑上，我在小型 VPS 上单独写了整个内容 - 创建盒子、Docker 与 systemd 服务，以及如何使用 SSH 隧道保持其私密性或使用防火墙锁定它：在 6 美元的 Droplet 上为你的 AI 代理提供自托管可观察性。

blocks.html_en: Why DigitalOcean, since a few people asked

sentences.en: Why DigitalOcean, since a few people asked
sentences.zh: 为什么选择 DigitalOcean，因为有几个人问

blocks.html_en:
Because the tool's whole personality is "small and predictable," and that's
what I want from the box it runs on too.

sentences.en: Because the tool's whole personality is "small and predictable," and that's what I want from the box it runs on too.
sentences.zh: 因为该工具的整体个性是“小而可预测”，这也是我想要从它运行的盒子中得到的。

blocks.html_en:
A $6 Droplet runs this comfortably. There's no managed database bill on top,
because the database is a file — I back it up by copying the file or
snapshotting the Droplet, and I'm done. The pricing is flat and I can do the
math in my head, which matters when the whole point of the project is not
having a surprise infrastructure bill for a side project. I don't want to
find out at the end of the month that some egress line item ate the savings.

sentences.en: A $6 Droplet runs this comfortably.
sentences.zh: 6 美元的 Droplet 运行起来很舒服。

sentences.en: There's no managed database bill on top, because the database is a file — I back it up by copying the file or snapshotting the Droplet, and I'm done.
sentences.zh: 上面没有托管数据库账单，因为数据库是一个文件 - 我通过复制文件或拍摄 Droplet 快照来备份它，然后就完成了。

sentences.en: The pricing is flat and I can do the math in my head, which matters when the whole point of the project is not having a surprise infrastructure bill for a side project.
sentences.zh: 定价是固定的，我可以在脑海中进行数学计算，当项目的重点不是为副项目带来意外的基础设施账单时，这一点很重要。

sentences.en: I don't want to find out at the end of the month that some egress line item ate the savings.
sentences.zh: 我不想在月底发现某些出口订单项消耗了节省的资金。

blocks.html_en:
I've also just had a good time on it. The docs are readable, the Droplet
comes up in under a minute, and the Marketplace Docker image means "spin up a
box that can run a container" is a checkbox rather than an afternoon. For
something like this — one binary, one file, one small server sitting next to
your agents — it fits the shape of the thing. That's really the whole reason.

sentences.en: I've also just had a good time on it.
sentences.zh: 我也刚刚度过了一段愉快的时光。

sentences.en: The docs are readable, the Droplet comes up in under a minute, and the Marketplace Docker image means "spin up a box that can run a container" is a checkbox rather than an afternoon.
sentences.zh: 文档是可读的，Droplet 在不到一分钟内就会出现，而 Marketplace Docker 镜像意味着“启动一个可以运行容器的盒子”是一个复选框，而不是一个下午。

sentences.en: For something like this — one binary, one file, one small server sitting next to your agents — it fits the shape of the thing.
sentences.zh: 对于这样的事情——一个二进制文件、一个文件、一台位于代理旁边的小型服务器——它符合事物的形状。

sentences.en: That's really the whole reason.
sentences.zh: 这确实是全部原因。

blocks.html_en:
Anyway. If you've been squinting at agent logs, go self-host something and
stop. It doesn't have to be mine, but it turns out it really doesn't need to
be complicated.

sentences.en: Anyway.
sentences.zh: 反正。

sentences.en: If you've been squinting at agent logs, go self-host something and stop.
sentences.zh: 如果您一直在查看代理日志，请自行托管一些内容并停止。

sentences.en: It doesn't have to be mine, but it turns out it really doesn't need to be complicated.
sentences.zh: 它不一定是我的，但事实证明它确实不需要复杂。

content_html_zh:
我建立了小型法学硕士代理。 不是您在演示中看到的令人印象深刻的那种，只是回答支持问题或挖掘一些文档并返回答案的实用小东西。 几个月来，最烦人的部分不是模型或提示。 我不知道那东西到底在做什么。

你知道这种感觉。 跑步需要九秒，但你不知道为什么。 它调用了一个工具，然后再次调用，然后是第三次，然后就在其中的某个地方花费了四十美分。 日志是一堵 JSON 墙。 你调整一个提示，发布它，直到用户抱怨之前，你真的无法判断你让它变得更好还是更糟。 我一直眯着眼睛看着 print() 输出，就像 2015 年一样。

所以我去找一些东西来向我展示我的代理运行情况。 这些工具是存在的——它们很好，有些真的很好——但它们都想要我不想提供的东西。

一直困扰我的两件事

托管者希望您在他们的服务器上留下痕迹。 这意味着您的提示，对我来说意味着实际的客户消息，位于其他人的数据库中，以便我可以查看仪表板。 不，谢谢。

自托管的程序是为公司构建的，这一点也表明了这一点。 每个人都推荐的需要 Postgres、ClickHouse 和 Redis 以及 S3 存储桶才能运行。 四个有状态的服务。 我在一个业余项目中每天可能会记录几千个模型调用。 建立一个 ClickHouse 集群来查看这一点就像租一辆叉车来搬运一袋杂货。

我坐了一会儿，意识到不匹配就是整个问题所在。 这不是公司规模的数据。 这是我的笔记本电脑规模的数据。 一旦你接受了这一点，设计就会变得非常无聊：它只是一个带有 SQLite 文件的 Web 应用程序。 就是这样。 一个过程。

所以我建造了它。 它的名字叫奥特望远镜。

它实际上是什么

这是一个 Go 二进制文件。 您运行它，它会打开一个 SQLite 文件，并侦听标准端口上的 OpenTelemetry 跟踪。 您使用一个环境变量（与每个 OpenTelemetry 设置已使用的环境变量相同）将代理指向它，然后您的运行就会开始显示。 没有帐户，没有配置文件，没有其他服务可以照顾。

我最关心的是数据的形状。 其他人都向您展示了 span，这是原始的 OpenTelemetry 单元，它读起来就像堆栈跟踪。 我想要跑步。 一次代理执行就是一次运行，在其中您可以看到以下步骤：此 LLM 调用、该工具调用、调用同一工具 3 次的循环。 点击进入通话，您可以阅读进入和返回的真实消息，其中包含令牌计数和成本。 当事情发生偏向时，你实际上可以看到哪里。

有一个成本表了解主要提供商的当前价格，因此运行会告诉您它的成本，而不是仅燃烧了多少代币。 如果它击中了我没有价格的型号，它会向您显示代币，并诚实地表示不知道金额，这比听起来更重要 - 我宁愿看到空白而不是虚构的数字。

然后是我最喜欢的部分：评估与痕迹住在同一个地方。 你写一张支票（“答案永远不应该包含‘我无能为力’”）或将一个法学硕士作为评判者指向运行，结果就会被印在实际的生产运行上。 大多数工具使评估成为针对单独数据集运行的单独产品。 这里只是一个关于您已经运行的列。 还有一个比较视图，所以“我的迅速改变是否让上周比前一周更糟糕”是一个你实际上可以通过观察而不是通过共鸣来回答的问题。

真正烦人的部分

如果你构建其中之一，没有人会警告你：人工智能跟踪没有单一格式。 至少有三个，现在都生活在野外，但他们的意见不一样。

OpenTelemetry 有一个“GenAI”约定，只是它处于迁移中期，因此有一个旧的属性布局和一个新的属性布局，并且两者都由当今的真实工具发出。 然后Arize的OpenInference是它自己的东西，它是OpenAI Agents SDK和一堆LangChain世界所说的东西。 然后，Vercel AI SDK 会执行自己的 ai.* 属性，并且（这是有趣的）它的跨度将新旧 OpenTelemetry 样式混合在同一个跨度中，因此，如果您根据一个属性进行猜测，您就会出错。

因此，Otterscope 的很大一部分只是一个翻译层，它吞噬所有这些并将它们转变为一个干净的模型。 它是项目中最不引人注目的代码，但可能也是最有价值的，因为这意味着您不必关心您的框架使用哪种方言。 而且因为我保留了所有进来的原始有效负载，当我稍后改进翻译时，您可以通过新版本重播旧数据，并且回溯性会变得更好。 那感觉值得做。

它不是什么

现在是单用户。 没有登录、没有团队、没有角色。 这是故意的——我建造了我需要的东西，而且我是一个人。 它默认绑定到本地主机，这样你就不会意外暴露它，如果你想要一个具有真正身份验证的团队版本，这是一个“当有人真正询问时”功能，而不是“根据规范构建它，因为电子表格说 B2B”功能。

也很年轻。 它会有粗糙的边缘。 但它做到了我想要的事情，那就是停止盲目飞行。

尝试一下

最快的方法是 Docker：

docker run -p 8317:8317 -p 4318:4318 -v otterscope:/data ghcr.io/otterscope/otterscope

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

然后将任何 OpenTelemetry 检测的代理指向 http://localhost:4318 并打开 http://localhost:8317。如果您只想先单击一下，有一个示例命令可以用假运行填充它。 该存储库包含 Pydantic AI、OpenAI Agents SDK、LangGraph 和 Vercel AI SDK 的设置片段。

回购协议在这里：https://github.com/otterscope

如果你更愿意在服务器上运行它而不是在笔记本电脑上，我在小型 VPS 上单独写了整个内容 - 创建盒子、Docker 与 systemd 服务，以及如何使用 SSH 隧道保持其私密性或使用防火墙锁定它：在 6 美元的 Droplet 上为你的 AI 代理提供自托管可观察性。

为什么选择 DigitalOcean，因为有几个人问

因为该工具的整体个性是“小而可预测”，这也是我想要从它运行的盒子中得到的。

6 美元的 Droplet 运行起来很舒服。 上面没有托管数据库账单，因为数据库是一个文件 - 我通过复制文件或拍摄 Droplet 快照来备份它，然后就完成了。 定价是固定的，我可以在脑海中进行数学计算，当项目的重点不是为副项目带来意外的基础设施账单时，这一点很重要。 我不想在月底发现某些出口订单项消耗了节省的资金。

我也刚刚度过了一段愉快的时光。 文档是可读的，Droplet 在不到一分钟内就会出现，而 Marketplace Docker 镜像意味着“启动一个可以运行容器的盒子”是一个复选框，而不是一个下午。 对于这样的事情——一个二进制文件、一个文件、一台位于代理旁边的小型服务器——它符合事物的形状。 这确实是全部原因。

反正。 如果您一直在查看代理日志，请自行托管一些内容并停止。 它不一定是我的，但事实证明它确实不需要复杂。
